What an honor!
|

Peter,

I have exciting news to share with you!

I was just informed that I got the second-most U.S. Senate votes in MNGOP history.

[ ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=de9a83b3a3cc4f0cb140557ca424ec9c&_e=LCS_RAsIvRaPK4PxBixA0rSr6B8b-7ilXQsf14AwfzhxUUfQIU86L21ve1O2JjFcbEthT91uA1b1Gk7f08J1KitUH4cdnIW6cBpNFlmmZmKSpSZ3rnNOmHrFbHkmFVl6G_0wv8A2HFHpFZbRVoa-VJYMyaSvEpE9hbCBQJ2gnL5BnKKOHc7lcffreXxuFtQav-HR10uxlv7bOqFl7MfTbHCl1_r5U_RxiIXr66agX-YaqChW7PvnKw9wpojdB_ha6Tkn5bdvNUG2YIrbHEPJhGj-BpXE6DiYMHUcxqSS20l9wqfPmxUsePU-dXRD8YQIWmEVG-MnxWAig_bXQY3hRw%3D%3D)
This happened without help; it was all done by the people. Wow, what an honor!

We're just getting started. Spread the word because we're turning this campaign right around. We will not stop until we flip MN red!

Our work starts right now. [****](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=de9a83b3a3cc4f0cb140557ca424ec9c&_e=LCS_RAsIvRaPK4PxBixA0rSr6B8b-7ilXQsf14AwfzhxUUfQIU86L21ve1O2JjFcbEthT91uA1b1Gk7f08J1KitUH4cdnIW6cBpNFlmmZmKSpSZ3rnNOmHrFbHkmFVl6G_0wv8A2HFHpFZbRVoa-VJYMyaSvEpE9hbCBQJ2gnL5BnKKOHc7lcffreXxuFtQaYqYCqhtRo88XGaOTRCLFoZdF8jtet-BUUXZVQXq_790YtSojq8WZImnwyGEMFQ0DiE59qZD9rYiDD95QuFCFAfiuY7W_BrL0yfvNBGeZnDzO_xmf1mnpZcglwDsUG8yA4HTg2xW3opo5MW1rEdPwEA%3D%3D)Would you be willing to donate $10 or more before midnight?

We proved that Minnesota is not a lost cause in 2024. Now, I'm working tirelessly to flip Minnesota RED once and for all.

Royce White

[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=de9a83b3a3cc4f0cb140557ca424ec9c&_e=LCS_RAsIvRaPK4PxBixA0rSr6B8b-7ilXQsf14AwfzhxUUfQIU86L21ve1O2JjFcbEthT91uA1b1Gk7f08J1KitUH4cdnIW6cBpNFlmmZmKSpSZ3rnNOmHrFbHkmFVl6G_0wv8A2HFHpFZbRVoa-VJYMyaSvEpE9hbCBQJ2gnL5BnKKOHc7lcffreXxuFtQaP4yeFcNgYxYKBIsAPwbEIrW7BJ4Exh70rGXYR3HxlWYpT3kSOmMeuJmN0ZN4JnnaLYllA5qtaMHWVQ7JMYi-KL_k0eZvQr0LSC4954WY3ISPZvkbEkLvX_pNa2fywJoUR0cva-5qKjbEKN_vGw66Rg%3D%3D)

Meet Royce White:

**Born and raised in the Twin Cities, Royce White was the son of a single mother and started playing sports at the age of 5 years old. Living in various neighborhoods growing up, much of his family remained in the Rondo community, where he spent much of his time and received schooling. White excelled at basketball and recieved a scholarship to play basketball at the University of Minnesota, and later transferred to Iowa State. He would go on to make Dean's List while receiving All Big-12 first-team honors and many other basketball awards.

_After being selected 16th overall by The Houston Rockets in the NBA draft, White began to voice concerns about the NBA 's collective bargaining agreement (CBA), specifically regarding mental health policy, which at the time did not exist. Eventually, his concerns became a public dispute between White and the NBA. This resulted in White demanding that the NBA, as a representative of the global corporate community, take up the responsibility of spearheading the mental health conversation - not just posturing through press statements, but through policy._

Royce then went on to win two championships in Canada before moving over to play Big 3 Basketball. White appeared at a game in a t-shirt that read "Free The Uyghurs." After a game-winning basket, White did a post-game interview on CBS, where he called for 2 Million Uyghurs to be freed and released from concentration camps. As the season went on, White would touch on taboo topics such as Gain of Function Research, The Federal Reserve, Julian Assange, Edward Snowden, Larry Hoover, Jeff Fort, Muammar Gaddafi, The Great Reset, Ivermectin, Me Too, etc.

**Now, Royce White is working tirelessly to FLIP Minnesota Red and give President Trump the MAGA reinforcements he needs. Can you help him by clicking the button below?**

** [**PASS ROYCE AN ASSIST**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=de9a83b3a3cc4f0cb140557ca424ec9c&_e=LCS_RAsIvRaPK4PxBixA0rSr6B8b-7ilXQsf14AwfzhxUUfQIU86L21ve1O2JjFcbEthT91uA1b1Gk7f08J1KitUH4cdnIW6cBpNFlmmZmKSpSZ3rnNOmHrFbHkmFVl6G_0wv8A2HFHpFZbRVoa-VJYMyaSvEpE9hbCBQJ2gnL5BnKKOHc7lcffreXxuFtQaBWDaFcaM29ss8s2gPYW_8Eay2wC7-1krVi1Ao_l09_zrBb9ZB6_N18AfiKkhjivYu7LgWyoSc4-8lO0eag4F0AHBbPsUvtMskTovbnTOA6OXwcXnmKFEu9OoLFRx61pzkJlsEBJiRO7Xh9_B-cMm3A%3D%3D)

The first $41,300/$15,000 from a person/multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to the DNC. The next $3,300/$5,000 from a person/PAC will be allocated to Harris for President’s Recount Account. The next $510,000/$255,000 from a person/PAC will be split equally among the Democratic state parties from these states: AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, VT, WA, WI, WV, and WY. Any additional funds will be allocated to the DNC, subject to applicable contribution limits.
A contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant by contacting **info@kamalaharris.com**.The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
clean_body: | | | Peter, we wanted to check in and give a short update before the week begins. Over the past few weeks, we have been in the states helping finish out recounts and making sure that every vote is counted. But the reality is, we need to see another surge of donations to allow us to continue building the foundation of resources needed to hold Trump accountable these next four years. If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | Thank you for doing your part, Kamala HQ | | | |

name: Jenna Burns
subject: She’s prosecuting Trump AND my husband
body:
Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way... life in prison could be his reward.
Dear Peter,

If the name Fani Willis sounds familiar to you, there's a reason.

She's the radical District Attorney in Georgia who's been trying to put President Trump behind bars.

But President Trump isn't the only person Fani Willis is abusing her office to target.

She's also trying to lock my husband away for the _rest_ _of_ _his_ _life_ for doing his job as a police officer and trying to keep our city safe.

**[Before I ask for your help, let me start at the beginning...](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHw_Rv9Av1YiwgVKvI1bu0OMxMif5PoVQMY6aQSkziMjHBBd0tn4e9loPwGRH-guWEdC3MkhiSKoZoirQ8pN5qEds6RfPTEG9QPqLn21M_hz2X0oaF03hfZtqzGNIFbKXkOvGCl5q1hQ33hgsGxBAk_j)**

My husband, James, served in the Atlanta Police Department. That's me, James, and our son in the photo I sent.
[](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHyKH5qk4ZLzVXBPT7bW-DzzfE_ItWnvt6B5_pVkSbZWceK-ztTQq8vYtTBOX5lsq7Mfv_WDNyr4Co4KG3lhhovWwR5VEwKQwNRa9QKSU5M_1T2rYb8Ts6_ANcZIVxEGB33Sr-yCJInhc5xm_u_hNgY6)
Being a police officer meant the world to James. He was born and raised in Atlanta, and saw his job as a way to help his neighbors. But if Fani Willis has her way... **_life in prison_** could be his reward.

Our nightmare started more than eight years ago, when an off-duty police officer radioed in about a man breaking into a parked car at an apartment complex.

Then the officer stopped responding. Dispatch issued a distress call, and all available units rushed over.

My husband was closest to the scene, so he arrived first. When he got there, he saw a running car parked facing the wrong way on a one-way street. With his emergency lights on, James got out to investigate.

That's when it happened.

The suspect saw my husband, slammed on the gas, and drove straight at him.

James was certain the man was intentionally trying to run him over.

With only a split second to think, James drew his weapon and fired a single shot at the vehicle bearing down on him. It struck the suspect, who died instantly.

James was shaken by the incident, but thankfully he was unhurt. He went home, certain he had done everything by the book. **So you can imagine our shock when James found out he was being charged with murder!**

James was forced to turn himself in, and spent the night locked up in jail in solitary confinement. They treated him like a common criminal. They even kept him in a straitjacket!

We all thought this was a giant misunderstanding -- just a soft-on-crime District Attorney trying to make a name for themselves by targeting a police officer.

We couldn't have been more wrong.

You see, James is white, and the suspect he shot was black. So the woke, anti-police prosecutors in Atlanta have made it their mission to destroy my husband.

For eight years now, these radical prosecutors -- and the woke mob who helped get them elected -- have dragged my husband's name through the mud.

The District Attorney at the time held press conferences bragging about how tough they were for going after my husband. They even campaigned on putting my James behind bars!

Every time James has to appear at the Fulton County Courthouse for a hearing, angry protesters show up carrying giant signs with James' mugshot on them, with “MURDERER” scribbled across his face in red spray paint.

Now you understand why I'm so upset and why **[I'm turning to you for help.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHzmpATshDh7CB4pNBsnkWPAQY3ro-sqIDhc-AuDJPm7TaZ1yJzBJOWuOhsFqybih3LZ-9JFrvDlZb4LtRaMsvehGKRXum3By12_BwsGPICbkEr8F-HryeMZsZTgnbW1WVZj3emj6ZSyyxS2X_FU4J46)**

James is an honest, hardworking, good-hearted man who only wants to help people.

He's the guy everyone calls when a broken faucet floods their kitchen, or when their car breaks down.

When he was younger, he spent years traveling to dangerous Third-World countries to help needy people -- places like South Sudan, Madagascar, Liberia, and Haiti. And it was that same selflessness that drove James to become a police officer and graduate the police academy near the top of his class.

My husband is _exactly_ the kind of man you want keeping your city safe.

But none of that matters to radical prosecutors like Fani Willis. She _knows_ what happened that night. She _knows_ James was in a life-or-death situation. She _knows_ that the charge should have been dropped years ago.

But Fani Willis doesn't care.

She just wants to make an example out of my husband. For years, we've been trapped in limbo.

James' police department just threw us away. There's supposed to be this bond of brotherhood in law enforcement. But his fellow officers were forbidden to talk to him -- and the higher-ups fired him before the investigation was even finished.

We've lost nearly all our friends, and we've even been forced to move because of the terrible lies the prosecutors have been spreading about my husband.

It's been nearly impossible for James to get a job with these charges hanging over him.

By some miracle, we've been able to get by, but every day is difficult. And what's been truly overwhelming is the cost of James' legal defense.

My friend, we aren't wealthy people. James' father had to empty his retirement account. Other relatives have sold family heirlooms. We've put nearly every penny we can scrape together into this case.

And it still hasn't been enough.

I know this is how Fani Willis and the radical prosecutors in her office are trying to punish my husband. They're dragging this out as long as possible to break our family.

Earlier this year, we ran out of money. James was about to turn to a public defender. We had all but given up hope... **And then we got a call from Jason Johnson, the President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund, or LELDF.**

If you've ever heard of LELDF, you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongfully-accused police officers like James.

LELDF has helped dozens of innocent officers -- and their families – at their lowest moment.

And we _were_ at our lowest moment!

Not only did the LELDF board decide to help my husband get the legal defense he deserves, they're also raising money to pay for the best attorneys so James can finally clear his name.

That's why I'm writing to you today to ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone...

**[Could you send a contribution today to help defend James?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHw_OcVD3lgdoFgLUq9ZidWdi8gLC9H-QY_Ri12-KEPpA7Xn7q2jBA7Ia-nEu2nTvk1W3-4px7OOPghtOYbgKhx_8wnKjwfOqcUGTpGxiwCRnYwLGhjHzFLGwjzK6_8SQfyFPumlumkeP2YKJwpnjq4T)**

James' trial is scheduled for November. His legal team needs to be working day and night to get ready to defeat Fani Willis's team and get my husband declared NOT GUILTY once and for all.

I know that times are tough right now. And if we had any other options, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for help from a stranger.

But like I mentioned earlier, we have nowhere else to turn. And I'm so scared for what will happen to my husband.

You know how police officers are treated behind bars. If James is convicted, it's basically a death sentence.

**[So, if you're at all able, would you consider making a tax-deductible donation of $35, $50, $100, or even $500 or $1,000 to LELDF to help wrongfully-accused officers like James?](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHxBtMteqZhgvjNRCTDjGmS2MbDvdCR_yFzs_cwIxwoAs1W3piXnDNg7Qlb2WbSeOl3Dh-7Hg9IC0nkumUbyxfje_y2usCrBQnxyyARxGCWTQKLklmRrui1n3yIL7HbLVILn1I-CK2obrJOHdeXY6Uvb)**

LELDF is a non-profit organization. So your gift is tax-deductible.

And it's your **[$35 gift](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHwnO3dHl0Iwkm8ZNjePN7cE1iSJEik-dZg3Db0BK5laXxLwno036DKg-GeFNfKu5YsbMRnmZCnn86ljiLoEx-33SGbZTws9oJnemPGmASs_FdQhPBWdsxxsd32zPeSzLRv18XACg4YkdnrlbMTvQjUZ)** that could make the difference between defeating Fani Willis and the radical anti-police prosecutors on her team -- and my husband spending the _rest_ _of_ _his_ _life_ in prison.

I'm sorry this e-mail turned out so long. Thank you for taking the time to read about my husband. And thank you in advance for helping my James.

Sincerely,
Jenna Burns

P.S. My husband did EVERYTHING he needed to survive that night. He doesn't deserve to die in prison. But that's EXACTLY what will happen if radical prosecutor Fani Willis gets her way. Please help James before it's too late! **[Follow this link right now to make your best tax-deductible gift of $35 or more to LELDF.](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET2DztDNxdcCYPPCCFI0lTUARO7XGAle0siobok5kxdt7-hFDd8nXRmB-YTJFzwrtEYTNsTZFyefGQ66eETM270ajAJ9uKUxEtmsS_3_2pcdvn5T97tZpobnCNwdStIroRR4bzvMSP_hQl1vvgk2IKpq3L-CISN-rB81J4gIU64ZOACwKQegcr6B5LkbHtIy27G69GoLSzkPs7eMOMODnTHyELB58AJTuYbyVAxfzrBs6cVy2d7nNEYhBhu_R6j04WPDaI-8bP_bVgCsRu5XsDz_xrDt7ImbRhr-ElDEpVJyg-pL5VHvwwhwCyCevue80KiC8TILFPTIPlPAHokFhpwVmi_C1SDSGI_uZEmOUQEec)** Thank you for helping me save my husband and my family.
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit**www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_
_(C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/u/click?_t=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_m=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_e=0rnlY1ddrvFHVV-kchqET4NXdRyvDfWCLTOb65-xASLDq7I71wPX-dTgX-2ETpYQgfLBgO6Sqr1RosZP0lTbco2QEbbWrzvtbmPRY_gzXn37zBNMd3w8C6LhX4CrQBwOffRQoaxuon_yF5BLY4lR6maF9q_VUlLYbV-kIrTPTHgD4Jx28gM7UfZieySQqPI4-o_vxYXB4QmoKGJRJwb81A%3D%3D)
2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303_

Click [here](http://links.itsourgreatamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=442fb7cf105f4725a8bda0b6bcffc1bd&_s=c8b12c3b39e4429795c3d07a60d87782&_t=qGIfab_MFbvdlTn2zawx6AozfRAuUNqYftHJNW992TCyCHT_8taw6HPl4kkW72TbSYjIveqJU3ObQK_Ra9DJ0Rijo0Y9mM9sI44VCLUPrdfqbg9T3rEco2m_Wq-4x90U) to unsubscribe

# **_Should I wear my Victory Hat on Inauguration Day?_**

[](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kdGOnCAUhp8G75joQUEvvJjWtU0nabppm03mxlA4ruwCEkUns0_faO3c_nzn8H8gXbCmv9dEfCIAg9EzASDsTADOz_Dy8xc-oaAf_DJS-YTfvm-n8PlAV3jAXsn3Zz5Uns3lzMsvv524vq_ZA4_3gA_4uPSEThq7paJJdA2qBKUSrDPBIK9KnqbJUKeSFQgCMlFlVS8LFHlapciYYEWpiyIxNaSQZxkUaQYZ8JPKNWNM6AqVFupPQfL0ZvxJj15a_RanxYWTGl1i6yHGMO-VWgLtjGqZ8HQzfkK9EQTanaZeRrNNUzU6Z2JEpG-9ItDaQIee7hp0NSqO050OMtI1J6xdouscarM4wppDlW_hPC6TQsIaGzobuqE_ciVdkObVE9YcSnkXveqOzf8Wd0Pf6b1WZ8P_wdFH9JGwZpOMSIB33hpNWNO-XC8_5o8r53u4fzBrKs_Xr2dZXJKp1uFmrDUzFyRPX7ea--usNfwNAAD__wP7qlg)

We’ve sold thousands - and I mean thousands - of these hats since you voted to make me the 47th President. So here’s the plan: if you think I should wear my Victory Hat on Inauguration Day, grab one for yourself, and we could match on the big day! you please order yours before the end of the day so we can get it to you in time? name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 11/24/2024
body:
Mayor Lied In Hate Crime Hoax Probe But DOJ Refused To Charge Him, FBI Official Says
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**November 24, 2024** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear friends,
This week, we need to talk about a deeply disturbing story that hasn’t made the headlines it deserves but has significant implications for leadership, trust, and election integrity. **Federal court documents have revealed that Colorado Springs Mayor Yemi Mobolade allegedly communicated with a suspect tied to a hate crime hoax during his 2023 mayoral campaign.** This hoax involved a [staged burning cross and racial slurs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) designed to create fear and division in the community while influencing voters during the election.

What’s most alarming is the reported involvement of the Department of Justice (DOJ), which declined to pursue charges despite evidence suggesting the mayor lied to federal investigators during the probe. This raises serious questions not just about the local leadership but also about the selective enforcement of justice. While the hoax may have been framed as a response to supposed hate, it’s clear that the intent was to manipulate public perception and sway an election—a betrayal of public trust at the highest level.

At Rocky Mountain Voice, we believe stories like this need to be told. Integrity in our elections and the honesty of our leaders are not optional; they are **foundational to our democracy**. That’s why we’re committing to digging deeper into this incident. This week, we’ll publish an in-depth article that examines the broader implications of this case, including the potential erosion of public trust and what it means for Colorado’s political landscape moving forward.

We are incredibly grateful for your continued support, which allows us to report on stories like this that others might overlook. Together, we’re holding leaders accountable and ensuring that Colorado remains a place where truth and integrity matter.

Stay tuned for our upcoming coverage, and thank you for being part of this journey. Let’s start this week committed to uncovering the facts and fighting for the values we hold dear.

In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl

---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[Tina Peters appeal filed while justice awaits Jena Griswold](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The legal journey of Tina Peters, former Clerk and Recorder of Mesa County, Colorado, has become emblematic of the contentious debates surrounding election integrity and judicial fairness. Peters now seeks bond pending appeal, citing significant procedural and constitutional concerns in her trial.

**[Boebert’s ZIP code bill boosting rural towns passes House Oversight Committee with unanimous support](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Rural America is home to nearly 60 million individuals. That’s one out of every five Americans. Many argue that policymakers overlook rural America. Yet a simple bill led by U.S. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Silt) will have a big impact on small towns.

**[Daniel: Eventually, you run out of other people’s money](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado, long admired for its breathtaking landscapes, vibrant outdoor culture, and once-thriving economy, finds itself at a crossroads.

|
---
**STATE**
**[Colorado voters approve Prop. KK, imposing new 6.5% excise tax on firearms and ammunition sales](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) On Nov. 5, 2024, Colorado voters approved Prop. KK, a ballot measure that introduces a 6.5% excise tax on the sale of firearms, ammunition and specific firearm parts.

**[Western Colorado ranchers bracing for impact of the next release of wolves](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Sen. Perry Will told his constituents in a social media post “Brace yourselves, Garfield County,” after Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) announced that they will begin phase two of their wolf reintroduction program, adding insult to injury to western Colorado ranchers who have already seen the devastating impact on livestock on their northern neighbors.

**[Lawmakers grill Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold about possible security breach](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado lawmakers grilled Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold on Friday at the state capitol about her handling of a possible security breach. Hundreds of passwords to election equipment were leaked days before Election Day and could have severely impacted half of Colorado’s counties.
**[Colorado Gov. Jared Polis, lawmakers spar over proposed budget](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[COLORADO POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The Colorado legislature’s budget drafting body, stung by criticism from the governor over hiking Medicaid provider rates last spring higher than what he wanted, bit back when Gov. Jared Polis met with the committee last week to discuss his 2025-26 budget proposal.

**[‘Like Tiananmen square’: Denver mayor vows city police, population will forcibly resist Trump deportation measures](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston vowed that his city’s police and population would resist President-elect Donald Trump’s deportation agenda in an interview Tuesday with the Denverite while comparing Trump and federal deportations to the Chinese Communist Party.

_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Musk and Ramaswamy: The DOGE plan to reform government](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE WALL STREET JOURNAL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Our nation was founded on the basic idea that the people we elect run the government. That isn’t how America functions today. Most legal edicts aren’t laws enacted by Congress but “rules and regulations” promulgated by unelected bureaucrats—tens of thousands of them each year.
**[Matt Gaetz withdraws name from consideration as Attorney General](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE DAILY CALLER](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Republican Florida Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew his name Thursday from consideration as attorney general. Gaetz wrote on X that his confirmation was “unfairly becoming a distraction” to the work of President-elect Donald Trump’s transition.
**[House Speaker bans men from women’s bathrooms on Capitol Hill](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE POST MILLENNIAL](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) House Speaker Mike Johnson has issued a new edict saying that only women shall have permission to access women’s bathrooms on Capitol Hill. The move from Johnson comes after South Carolina Rep Nancy Mace proposed a new rule that would deliver the same result Johnson has provided.

**[Jose Ibarra found guilty of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE DAILY WIRE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The illegal immigrant on trial for the murder of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been found guilty on all counts. Jose Ibarra, 26, was found guilty on 10 charges, including murder, kidnapping, and aggravated assault with intent to rape Riley, who was found murdered after she went jogging at the University of Georgia in Athens, the New York Post reported.

**[Prosecutors request stay in Trump NY case until 2029 as defense plans motion for dismissal ‘once and for all’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) New York prosecutors are requesting a stay until at least 2029 in New York v. Trump, as the president’s defense attorneys prepare to move to dismiss the case entirely.

**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Two more elementaries to close in Western Slope district, school board unanimously decides](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A special board meeting for Mesa County Valley School District 51 on Friday evening led to a 5-0 vote to close Clifton and Scenic Elementary Schools.

**[Mesa County commissioners updated on pretrial and corrections services programs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Matt Lewis, a former Mesa County sheriff and current Justice Services Director for the county, updated Mesa County commissioners on the status of their Pretrial Services (PTS) program and the County Community Corrections Services program on Tuesday, Nov. 19.

**[Western Colorado school board votes to close one elementary school, two more could shutter](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) It was an emotional night at the Mesa County Valley District 51 school board meeting on Nov. 19, as more than 50 public commenters packed a seven-hour session that stretched past midnight.

**[Denver has spent millions in voter-approved bond money to upgrade schools it might close](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CHALKBEAT COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Denver Public Schools has spent $22.6 million in voter-approved bond dollars over the past four years on schools that district officials are now considering closing or downsizing, according to information obtained by Chalkbeat in an open records request.

**[‘Something needs to be done’: Smash-and-grab burglaries on the rise](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[CBS COLORADO](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Law enforcement agencies are seeing a spike in smash-and-grab burglaries across the northern part of the Denver metro area and up into Northern Colorado.

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _A second Trump presidency has sparked significant concerns among some Colorado Democrats. They cite fears over federal overreach, social policy rollbacks and economic instability._ __

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The Durango 9-R School Board is poised to take an alarming step that undermines the values of neutrality and inclusivity our schools should uphold._ __
**[Verhoeff: Are veterans being exploited by companies claiming to support them?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Veterans are pillars of our society, honored for their sacrifices and service. Companies like USAA and Navy Federal Credit Union have built their brands around serving military families, often positioning themselves as trusted allies._ __

**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _A U.S. senator, Chris Coons (D-DE), said again he is “amused” that it will take two billionaires to oversee President-elect Donald Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency! (D.O.G.E.)_ __
**[Dickenson: From the Rockies to the Beltway, Colorado’s influence in Washington](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Colorado has long punched above its weight in shaping national policy, and this administration is no different. With the appointment of Chris Wright as Secretary of Energy, our state once again finds itself in a key position of influence in Washington, D.C._ __

clean_body: Mayor Lied In Hate Crime Hoax Probe But DOJ Refused To Charge Him, FBI Official Says | | | | November 24, 2024 SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST By Heidi Ganahl | | Dear friends, This week, we need to talk about a deeply disturbing story that hasn’t made the headlines it deserves but has significant implications for leadership, trust, and election integrity. Federal court documents have revealed that Colorado Springs Mayor Yemi Mobolade allegedly communicated with a suspect tied to a hate crime hoax during his 2023 mayoral campaign. This hoax involved a designed to create fear and division in the community while influencing voters during the election. What’s most alarming is the reported involvement of the Department of Justice (DOJ), which declined to pursue charges despite evidence suggesting the mayor lied to federal investigators during the probe. This raises serious questions not just about the local leadership but also about the selective enforcement of justice. While the hoax may have been framed as a response to supposed hate, it’s clear that the intent was to manipulate public perception and sway an election—a betrayal of public trust at the highest level. At Rocky Mountain Voice, we believe stories like this need to be told. Integrity in our elections and the honesty of our leaders are not optional; they are foundational to our democracy. That’s why we’re committing to digging deeper into this incident. This week, we’ll publish an in-depth article that examines the broader implications of this case, including the potential erosion of public trust and what it means for Colorado’s political landscape moving forward. We are incredibly grateful for your continued support, which allows us to report on stories like this that others might overlook. Together, we’re holding leaders accountable and ensuring that Colorado remains a place where truth and integrity matter. Stay tuned for our upcoming coverage, and thank you for being part of this journey. Let’s start this week committed to uncovering the facts and fighting for the values we hold dear. In liberty and truth, Heidi Ganahl | | | TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK The legal journey of Tina Peters, former Clerk and Recorder of Mesa County, Colorado, has become emblematic of the contentious debates surrounding election integrity and judicial fairness. Peters now seeks bond pending appeal, citing significant procedural and constitutional concerns in her trial. Rural America is home to nearly 60 million individuals. That’s one out of every five Americans. Many argue that policymakers overlook rural America. Yet a simple bill led by U.S. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Silt) will have a big impact on small towns. Colorado, long admired for its breathtaking landscapes, vibrant outdoor culture, and once-thriving economy, finds itself at a crossroads. | STATE On Nov. 5, 2024, Colorado voters approved Prop. KK, a ballot measure that introduces a 6.5% excise tax on the sale of firearms, ammunition and specific firearm parts. Sen. Perry Will told his constituents in a social media post “Brace yourselves, Garfield County,” after Colorado Parks and Wildlife (CPW) announced that they will begin phase two of their wolf reintroduction program, adding insult to injury to western Colorado ranchers who have already seen the devastating impact on livestock on their northern neighbors. Colorado lawmakers grilled Colorado Secretary of State Jena Griswold on Friday at the state capitol about her handling of a possible security breach. Hundreds of passwords to election equipment were leaked days before Election Day and could have severely impacted half of Colorado’s counties. The Colorado legislature’s budget drafting body, stung by criticism from the governor over hiking Medicaid provider rates last spring higher than what he wanted, bit back when Gov. Jared Polis met with the committee last week to discuss his 2025-26 budget proposal. Democrat Denver Mayor Mike Johnston vowed that his city’s police and population would resist President-elect Donald Trump’s deportation agenda in an interview Tuesday with the Denverite while comparing Trump and federal deportations to the Chinese Communist Party. | NATIONAL Our nation was founded on the basic idea that the people we elect run the government. That isn’t how America functions today. Most legal edicts aren’t laws enacted by Congress but “rules and regulations” promulgated by unelected bureaucrats—tens of thousands of them each year. Republican Florida Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew his name Thursday from consideration as attorney general. Gaetz wrote on X that his confirmation was “unfairly becoming a distraction” to the work of President-elect Donald Trump’s transition. House Speaker Mike Johnson has issued a new edict saying that only women shall have permission to access women’s bathrooms on Capitol Hill. The move from Johnson comes after South Carolina Rep Nancy Mace proposed a new rule that would deliver the same result Johnson has provided. The illegal immigrant on trial for the murder of Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been found guilty on all counts. Jose Ibarra, 26, was found guilty on 10 charges, including murder, kidnapping, and aggravated assault with intent to rape Riley, who was found murdered after she went jogging at the University of Georgia in Athens, the New York Post reported. New York prosecutors are requesting a stay until at least 2029 in New York v. Trump, as the president’s defense attorneys prepare to move to dismiss the case entirely. | LOCAL A special board meeting for Mesa County Valley School District 51 on Friday evening led to a 5-0 vote to close Clifton and Scenic Elementary Schools. Matt Lewis, a former Mesa County sheriff and current Justice Services Director for the county, updated Mesa County commissioners on the status of their Pretrial Services (PTS) program and the County Community Corrections Services program on Tuesday, Nov. 19. It was an emotional night at the Mesa County Valley District 51 school board meeting on Nov. 19, as more than 50 public commenters packed a seven-hour session that stretched past midnight. Denver Public Schools has spent $22.6 million in voter-approved bond dollars over the past four years on schools that district officials are now considering closing or downsizing, according to information obtained by Chalkbeat in an open records request. Law enforcement agencies are seeing a spike in smash-and-grab burglaries across the northern part of the Denver metro area and up into Northern Colorado. | COMMENTARY A second Trump presidency has sparked significant concerns among some Colorado Democrats. They cite fears over federal overreach, social policy rollbacks and economic instability. The Durango 9-R School Board is poised to take an alarming step that undermines the values of neutrality and inclusivity our schools should uphold. Veterans are pillars of our society, honored for their sacrifices and service. Companies like USAA and Navy Federal Credit Union have built their brands around serving military families, often positioning themselves as trusted allies. A U.S. senator, Chris Coons (D-DE), said again he is “amused” that it will take two billionaires to oversee President-elect Donald Trump’s new Department of Government Efficiency! (D.O.G.E.) Colorado has long punched above its weight in shaping national policy, and this administration is no different. With the appointment of Chris Wright as Secretary of Energy, our state once again finds itself in a key position of influence in Washington, D.C. _ | Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com) or send us a message on social media to chat! | 🤔 DID YOU KNOW? The Liberty Bell has a typo on it. It features "Pensylvania" instead of "Pennsylvania," which, according to the National Science Foundation (NSF), isn't actually incorrect—multiple spellings of the state's name were commonly used at the time. | The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. | | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! Please follow Cliff Schecter on Twitter @cliffschecter, or subscribe to The Takedown with Cliff Schecter on YouTube, to keep up to date with this battle for the heart and soul of our country. — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. name: Hillsdale College via Conservative Intel
subject: Your Supreme Court survey
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vsReUpXW9N
Hillsdale College

Your participation in Hillsdale College's National Survey
on the United States Supreme Court..
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/osReUpXW9N

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Dear Friend,

Your participation in Hillsdale
College's National Survey on the United States Supreme Court is
requested.

Your response will only take a few
minutes, but the subject is crucial to the future of liberty and
the separation of powers in America.

The American Founders thought that the
proper role of the Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution.

But progressives see the Court
differently. They want activist judges to legislate from the
bench.

Do you agree that the role of the
Supreme Court is to apply the Constitution as it is written and
NOT to make laws or advance a particular policy agenda?

YES

NO

Even worse, progressives dismayed by
recent Supreme Court decisions want to "pack" the bench with
additional justices to advance a preferred policy agenda.

As Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) put
it in a Boston Globe op-ed: "To restore balance and integrity to
a broken institution, Congress must expand the Supreme Court by
four or more seats."

That is why I'm reaching out to you
today...

What is your opinion about the Supreme
Court's role in our constitutional republic?

Please share your thoughts with me
using the College's National Survey on the United States Supreme
Court right away.

Your response will help Hillsdale form
effective strategies to educate millions of Americans about the
importance of the Constitution to liberty-including the proper
role of an independent judiciary that applies the Constitution
instead of legislating from the bench.

You can easily complete your National
Survey on the United States Supreme Court using this secure link:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/63876934:sReUpXW9N:m:3:2532013465:0F3D1F8CE2AFC07947D80955A2379C84:r

Thank you for your time and
attention-and for your support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty
since 1844

Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI
49242 USA

This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com
You were added to the system January 18, 2022.
For more information please follow the URL below:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/isReUpXW9N

Follow the URL below to update your preferences or opt-out:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/osReUpXW9N

with "Unsubscribe" as the subject line.

## Texans Joining Trump Administration
President-elect Donald Trump selected two more Texans to join his administration when he takes office next January. Brooke Rollins, the president and CEO of the America First Policy Institute, has been nominated [to lead the Department of Agriculture](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGOszAQhJ_GdEbetR1C4eJv6PK3V6IFO8nqDEa2E8Lbn7j-uilGM5--r9t4I468Pv7TElz93NOICo0EkGhHTxyP0TTeTcr30AQHnUarjTaqeTpA8qhM8B6MBkKFFoyZCS9dN12vtmF3jgGgBcDO9i11kzXWapqt1ld9F0YFzzXltj5DDR8q93ikd8jtnJYmumetWxH6n8BB4LDve7ulyJXndBYEDmvYi8DhvBE4AJxZCxxqfi2bjOmgyKXKKaf0HWROMfJa5Kt4kiXMOVTKhyyvvGUuQa5p4ZUqp1UqBT3oHprs_LZzjFwunTDqsRDHX7r6p663w58AAAD__1QhbAg). She previously served as director of the Office of American Innovation and acting director of the Domestic Policy Council during the first Trump administration. Former NFL defensive back and state lawmaker Scott Turner has been nominated to [lead the Department of Housing and Urban Development](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLvO3CAQRp8Gd7MyA_hSUKRx96dNaWEDC1oMFoz38vbRpk_96Ts6On9-1h8TU8z33-Zwmt6-rNijBM4B1WpNTJ9VdlZvvZ155zQfBSohhey7oGfpvN_R7tO02X0e1ODsqCzabfZysGMX9RfGOSrOcVTzzYybkkoJsyshJuGZ7J2NVOqNgiP3Ns2nT3m6etvL0SUdiM7GxC-GC8OFggsxpe_GcAnlcNm9GsPF2CPm2KgaiiUzXFTfD4JPoIBCzPcGVOCRywvMVi4CX-rhKmSfoJGp0PZCBHTV7CpQvY6zwRn3x_eWnLEQLstw6aq25yumFNswMtnfDxP_2XT033RPjX8DAAD__xRVc2o). Turner is the chair of the Center for Education Opportunity at the America First Policy Institute, and he led the White House Opportunity and Revitalization Council during Trump’s first term. Meanwhile, Waco Rep. Pete Sessions is launching the [Delivering Outstanding Government Efficiency (DOGE) Caucus](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOtCAUBeCnwQ4jF_gdC4q_sZtttzRXuCoZQCM4jm-_cfvtTnKSLyfn-zk80Qef5i-MZMpnWgdoQHEhOOjBoQ_XoCpnxsZ1oiIjWglaKqmaajGdxU6AmpRy9Og02qZrHXYtahqbB0DlzY0JAVoIaHVXYztqpbVEq6V8yImphpwv616XhQp9ME_hWt-013aNVTBLKVtm8j-DnkF_nmf9GsvnLhn0t82gF-LOkkG_08Y3KsQz5ezXlHnAI9mFMp9vNEVKhdM0eesp2YtbPOyReT62bd2LTzOPR37xHSPmE-PFoK9247bTh-Dzv5apZo7ow--68uddbwM_AQAA__9DnW4O) in the U.S. House of Representatives, seeking to support Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy's cost-cutting efforts. Sen. John Cornyn joined the [upper chamber’s DOGE caucus](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLFuwyAUAL8Gb1jmPQhhYOjiLV07Whhw_BRsLEzs5O-rdO980p10P7fh5ijRev92S7T1NeUBOpBcCA5qCI7Se5BNsGMXjGiiFRpBoUTZNbMdUergYLrEC0aj3BhVGE1wTnTeIKqG7EcmBCghQCvTOj0qqRQ6rxCvODHZxUA1l7bOscaX26f0zkcsrc9Lk-xc67Yz_GLQM-jP82z9XPL6oQz6LSeq5HcGvSuVfIoMep_L-l758twf_KAjPnjI98hreS4bF8bgVWrdbvPWFBu2k1Ki_aKZ7O6Lo_TXrf-OOCz8BgAA__-S4GCw).
## 233,000 Medicaid Applications Overdue
The Texas Health and Human Services Commission (HHSC) has requested state funding to [hire nearly 1,800 additional employees](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2OwyAQhJ8Gd1j8npOC4hp3ufZKa80uMQo2EZA4efuT0189M59G3-9lukBMcbv-wEquvUKelFCGS8mVnRBiek-mQzcLPMuOnBy0stpoI7rFYdDCgzxTCF4IaYlg8Gaew8kCaDp10R0wKZWVUg323MMwW2OtBm-1PunAjCCMLZe-LdToBTWkd35S6X1eu-SW1u6V6W-mRqbGfd_7G7XbETI1brRXpsbPjKlxWarnUG-Vh1z48tiwEFaeA19jSjFvlbfMC-HDE18Jo4eIvG5w50gJ3gejKw7v-1GvXwMz4rpCTJ8v7V85T6f-AgAA__9a1mlf) and overhaul IT systems to address delays in processing Medicaid and Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) applications. Currently, [233,000 Medicaid applications are overdue](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7GO8yAQhJ_G7rBgF-y4oPibdPnbK63FLAk6giPDheTtT05_1TQz8-n7uiwXiinm63-6s62vsC0gQQulBJjFU0zvRffeOuln1bNVE4JBjVr2N-tOBsiN7NABjQQ6nGAdecYZp1Wi66M9zpQCoxRMZh5ockYbg7QaxBOGTkv2sW77UG9c-UUlpPf25H1Yt3uf7K3WR-nwXwfnDs6ttSFzK435-yh0cP5sRMn0EI4zh1iLaLTnmK8i_GR_ZNpKEWqeJcDY79Y_WkwplnHqtLzeKaYPrP5p_7TwGwAA__9NpFz_), with an average processing time of 71 days—far exceeding the federal requirement of 45 days. Between 2019 and 2023, HHSC processed SNAP applications within the required 30-day timeframe in only 5 of those 60 months. The agency has been under a federal Corrective Action Plan since 2020, and it [risks financial penalties](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO8yAQhJ8Gd1iwQOwUFH_jLn97pbWGJUGHITIkTt7-5EhXXjXFNzMazddlvmBMMV__40q2vUKZQYDmUnIws8eY3rPuvF2EP8uOrBwUGKWVFt3NBjeejT8Fo0kugx61O-kRtJKLEQfpoj3KpAQjJQzm3OOwGG2MQmeUGlVgWpCPrWx9u1GjF9aQ3uVJW-_K2iV7a-1emfrHYGIw7fvef78wH5DBlGmvDKZP7Ff5Sj46jJ7XjHfuKeG7clceyXNXauO1YSO-xpRiyZXHzAN52jDx8Mg-5iuDqdusv--HpZ4GpsV1xZg-i9qfFz0t_AQAAP__f5tqUQ) if it does not meet timeliness standards.
## Chuck Woolery Dies In Texas
Chuck Woolery, the iconic game show host known for "Love Connection," "Scrabble," and "Wheel of Fortune," [passed away at his home](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7GOtCAYAJ8GOgx8gGhB8Td2-7dXGoQPIYeHUW5d3_6y1189yUzm4zE_XC75a_3vNrTtFesMHBQTgoGeg8vlnhUNduFhFBStMBK0VFJxmqxZYtQIxhgjFzn6aDhXuIxDRO0HQJrtWyYEaCHA6LFzZtFKa-m8lnKQkSiOIbd6dC1hw5c7Y7nrE4_O140Wm1rbTyL_EZgITDvWveAbEZh8-vaf7Kq14HGz1W3IzlQvlurZWEAXmGtskGzoTT8KoIcN-5VLyWdviOLr5nL5rbQ_t58WfgIAAP__Z7lZSg) in Texas at the age of 83. Born in Ashland, Kentucky, Woolery hosted “Wheel of Fortune” from 1975 until 1982, when he was succeeded by Pat Sajak after a contract dispute. He later expressed [regret over his decision to leave the show](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGOhCAUheGn0Q4DFxAtKLaZbrbd0qBcBrIohrk7jm-_cfutv-Sc5P-6T3eXctoen25FS-9QJuCgmBAM9ORdyuekWm9n7kfRohVGgpZKKt5Gi3PgQXE5-yGAkYNQPfYjDno0Zljk0CZ7jQkBWggweuycmbXSWrpFSznI0CiOPlGpHUUkfLtnyGd5Ye2WsrbZRqL92ciPBm4N3I7j6E4XS7m0gRtuhJVc2lbc6PJ4siX-LN_sKCVjPVnFPbsFPdsdMdGLUfe9gS7Smttq_X6knNOzN43ij9Wl_HdL_3Z4WfgNAAD__5sAX70), telling the _New York Times_ in 2003 that he would have been making $10 million a year if he had remained its host. Woolery is best remembered for his role as host of the “Love Connection,” a show on which single men or women watched audition tapes of potential mates and then [picked](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGP3CAUBOBfg7tnwQOMKSjSuLu0KS0Mz2d0GE6GrHf_fbRR2rTfSCPN_PpYP3zKqXz-9Ce5_tzrihwVCAGo1-hTfq1qiG7j0YqBnDAStVRS8eFw3AZpvJ4na-xkgyUdTOTWRNQKZeRDcu8yIVALgUbb0ZtNK62lD1rKWe5McYqp12vsB3V6-rbnV33QNYZ6DtkdvX83Jn8wXBgu932PX1SafqcMF3_1FDIxXArdjeFCpdPVfSonlQ7_MBy_wxfctWa6XnDU1qHukOuDINRSKPRUC_gSoYXLb1smiIka-A6zZLho1GBNnGeUCsRuDaig8E0T7HrabYx28-iHy8XvO-Wc2mSY4p-nT_nvjv7fYx8O_wQAAP__t2t4xw)[ one for a date](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGP3CAUBOBfg7tnwQOMKSjSuLu0KS0Mz2d0GE6GrHf_fbRR2rTfSCPN_PpYP3zKqXz-9Ce5_tzrihwVCAGo1-hTfq1qiG7j0YqBnDAStVRS8eFw3AZpvJ4na-xkgyUdTOTWRNQKZeRDcu8yIVALgUbb0ZtNK62lD1rKWe5McYqp12vsB3V6-rbnV33QNYZ6DtkdvX83Jn8wXBgu932PX1SafqcMF3_1FDIxXArdjeFCpdPVfSonlQ7_MBy_wxfctWa6XnDU1qHukOuDINRSKPRUC_gSoYXLb1smiIka-A6zZLho1GBNnGeUCsRuDaig8E0T7HrabYx28-iHy8XvO-Wc2mSY4p-nT_nvjv7fYx8O_wQAAP__t2t4xw). In this role, he popularized the catchphrase "2 and 2," signaling a two-minute, two-second commercial break. Woolery was married several times and has five children.

President-elect Donald Trump completed his cabinet selections on Saturday, nominating former White House aide [Brooke Rollins](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAQRb8Gd1gwQIgLite4y2u3tDCMk9lgYwGOlb9fZfvtrnSlc6TzdZtunhJt9_9-RdeWPIEAzaXkYKboKb0n3UW3IAKqDp20CozSRg3dw1llZ2OsV9paY0XQVzOLMAiPVggbfUfuQ5MSjJRKmWt_scOslRmM0EYsCzAtMFLLpf_OtLUHLumdX1j6kNcuuUdre2XqH4ORwXieZx_muuFZPz-D8TMZjK0c6853Cs_K55LzE3nJKdFWub8XCkdqR0FeMRRsvrz5UaNnMHbFxf2klKheLNPivnpKv-r2V4qXg58AAAD__06cYXM) as agriculture secretary.

**Good Morning!** On this day in 1783, British troops [departed New York City](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFu7CAQheGngQ4LBljbBcVt3O1tU1qsGS-TYLMCZMtvH22UNvUnnSP9H_f57inR_vzvN3RtzTNIMEIpAXYOntI1Gx7cigioOTrVa7DaWD3y6EAb6WFZRhP6ISipwQ5W6mUwXt782HNy7zWlwCqltR26Wz8-jLajlcbKdQVmJAZquXSfmfYWcU1XPrB0S954crG1V2X6H4OJwXSeZxeptlyutzOYWqQqgr8E7eJXGEzJ1yYehRrVKGpOgbBUkdAfKHY8xZXLlwBeXHidlBLVW8-MfG6e0s9v-6vD4eA7AAD__90wX8I), ending their occupation and allowing General George Washington to lead the Continental Army’s triumphant return. Thanksgiving week is upon us, and to usher in the holiday season, we present this [charming video of two foxes](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0z7tOwzAYxfGnsbdW9ndpnMEDA9nKwsAYub4kRk5cJabQt0dhZzrSGX7S_-M6Xl0ueZ3e3BJtS3UEBXTS-gQ8BpfLcyQZbIoRIspodYfASIy9nG0I4J32IRmkviPvPXbhwuHYZNjLbA9Na2CtEdmcL11_I-SeFbFKCQSpGHKr2_mz5rXNMZVnfcTt7Osii51bu-8CXwQMAoaf4xUwvJZY17q591vcJgHD3lz72gUM2rAxpBRqQlRkwPRys-H-nUvJ-6UTpKbF5fKnt_9qHxZ-AwAA__9xdVOg) romping in the snow at Oxford's Magdalen College. ## Trump Finalizes Cabinet with Ag Pick
President-elect Donald Trump completed his cabinet selections on Saturday, nominating former White House aide [Brooke Rollins](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGO6yAQRb8Gd1gwQIgLite4y2u3tDCMk9lgYwGOlb9fZfvtrnSlc6TzdZtunhJt9_9-RdeWPIEAzaXkYKboKb0n3UW3IAKqDp20CozSRg3dw1llZ2OsV9paY0XQVzOLMAiPVggbfUfuQ5MSjJRKmWt_scOslRmM0EYsCzAtMFLLpf_OtLUHLumdX1j6kNcuuUdre2XqH4ORwXieZx_muuFZPz-D8TMZjK0c6853Cs_K55LzE3nJKdFWub8XCkdqR0FeMRRsvrz5UaNnMHbFxf2klKheLNPivnpKv-r2V4qXg58AAAD__06cYXM) as agriculture secretary. Rollins, a Texas [attorney with agricultural ties](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvwyAQRn-NvTmC4wB7YOiSLV07WhiO5FpsLEwS5d9X6d7x0yc9Pb2vy3zxnHm7fvqVXEtlBgE4SDmAnqPn_Jqxjy4RAamenLQKtEKtpv7mFrARMGgDGlEuBlJAkgJjNAInL3p2b5qUoKVUSo8nY6cFlZ60QC1Sgg4FRW6lnr4Lb-1GKb_Kg-oplLXP7tbafnTqo4NzB2e_b_Q83td71MYhUwfnpZbyQ0MtOfN2DK3e133w18rhntu90iDtRKM0SpsxWFA-kYlgRxwDCQwAfXVxf3LOfBjbobiunvOfQfuvyMPBbwAAAP__JYBgbw), has built a strong relationship with Trump since his first term in office. Trump's 15 cabinet nominees, spanning a mix of predictable and unexpected choices, now await Senate confirmation. Additionally, he has appointed several key administrative officials. Here's a full [roundup of all his nominees so far](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L1uwyAUxfGngY0ILmCbgaGLt3TtaGFziW_FR2TTRHn7Kt07H-l3pP_XdbkGylRvn6Gg76ktIMEIpQTYJQbKr8Xw6BMioObo1ajBamO147uXA9o4oZEqrYNWLm7jFgdcIbnBoVSc_FtTCqxSWtvpMoxuNdo6K42VKQEzEiP1dly-G9W-Y8qv9sDjsrXCs997v59MfzCYGcx9x51yfm8M5r0VrPg8GcwhFqp09iN0apXBbNxkJwmiHz_lLrawUsUuaitUEU9x4tZqFB2PN8QPH-9PypnOYWRG3kqgvxPe_wvy8PAbAAD__8mFYrM).
## Millennials Plan Holiday Splurge
Millennials are [leading holiday spending projections](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDFvwyAQhX8N3rDMAXEYGLp4S9eOFjFHfNUZIkNj5d9X7t7tk57ep6f3dZtvgZjy4zNs6FsqMwxgpFIS7BwD8Xs2XfQJEVB36NWowWpjtetWb-PFOZPSYDGO6joqDKCSdnpxMVxt6MifNqXAKqW1vfaX0d2Nts4Oxg4pgTADRmpl778L5bZi4nd54d4vZevYr609q9AfAiYB03Ec_ZLvyxkKmE61gEmpk0_aiBlzpsBVHsQs6xNzlHd6yLZSlWthiuEtK4Zasmx7yPUnU8kyUY61X9vG3e7j8yxTvYzCDI8tEP-taf-98_LwGwAA__8qemaT) this year, with 63% planning to spend the same or more compared to last year, according to a recent survey. Their younger Gen Z counterparts are showing more caution, with [71% expressing concerns about inflation's](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kD1vgzAUAH8NbEb-hDB46MKWrh2RYz_Dixwb-TmJ-PdVune94XS6n-t6dZgwb9_uAbbFskouNROCSbMGh-lcdR9sBJCgerBiUtIobdTc7zZw4Uc1m_mig3GKByUmw_3oRRyD8aJH-7EJIY0QSpnLME7zTSszG64Nj1F2mkPAVupwL5jbDjGd5QV18OXRJ7u3dlCnvjq5dHK5uye54e6GUrdOLhne1MnlqEDEKiRwBB9Az_qCk02ClcgaQCbmS_ZQMwTmbuXZGOaYXMOSWduR2F4SBncy2stxYN4YgaOS-2rD8caUkMap03x7OEx_Ze2_Uy8rfwMAAP__7spsvQ) impact on shopping. The National Retail Federation projects record holiday spending of nearly $989 billion between November and December, even as credit card debt tops $1.17 trillion. Gen Z shoppers are prioritizing clothing (62%) and gift cards (52%) for their purchases, while millennials are more likely to utilize "buy now, pay later services," which [experts predict](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j7Fu7CAQAL8Gd7bYBeznguI17i5tSsuY5dgIGwu4i-7vo0uffjSj-bytt40Tn_eP7SDbQl5Rou4BejSr3zi9Vt15G4iQVEcWJoVGaaPmLlqYAZyXQcoRR-N2dNMO-zR7qQmMHju2bxsAGgClzL9hnGanlZmN1EaGgEJL8txyGb4yny1SSK_8pDLs-eiSja1dVaj_AheBi3tUPqnWYfPZ0RsRuBSq-VF2qgKXmBP77dXXmK-Lz3tf6MqlDbEdqSvWX9-cEtdxElrej43Tb6b9tf20-BMAAP__4fdaPw) will hit a single-day record of $993 million on Cyber Monday.
## Biden to Pardon Two Turkeys as PETA Protests
President Joe Biden will pardon two turkeys today, enacting a [decades-long Thanksgiving tradition](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j7GOhCAUAL8GOgw8QKSguMZur73SgDyVPZSNsmv8-8tev_UkM5mf23DzKadt_vYrujqVATgoJgQDPUSf8jUoGt2ECCgpOmEkaKm0tHRxWhqPvhvRtCGOGKw3AjsRggo6WN_S5N42IUALIaXumtbYoKS2mivNpwmI4hhTLXtzL2mrC075Ki_cm7GsNLul1sdB5BeBnkB_nmdz-aWUNyXQb3geBPrsV2TxOf6ye0EWUsSNPfcZIxNWcd0Ct81S10x3Fx9nyjkdrSGKz6tP-T9UP42_HPwFAAD__wQqWa4) for his last time as President. People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals (PETA) has called the turkey pardon a “wretched” tradition, sending Biden a [letter](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLGSpCAURb8Gs2fJA7QNCDYx6003tBAeLdsoFmA7_fdTPfnE99apU-fffb6bEMP--Gs20tWnGTuUwDmgmp0J8T3LxmlPhCQa0nwQqIRUYmxWjcaRldL3HUnX20EIHNHaTkm6SdPbJugPjXNUnAuhbm0_jIsUalSdVJ33yGRHLtSU2_8p7HUlH9_pRbm1aWuiXms9ChN_GE4Mp-u62oOqaVN-MJw2csEwnHa6CmSKZAoVhtMSHO3wOcJxlpUKRLMRuNM-4chUPnOFmoB2B2YpZ3ZQz_ykNxwmu7TDQj5lgvQyEZL3wRLQV6gMpyZrd1whxlD6gcnusZkQf2Trb_FeGr8DAAD__xW2c9w) urging him to end the practice and citing turkey factory farming as “dirty business.” The two Minnesotan birds rescued from Thanksgiving dinner demise were dubbed [Peach and Blossom](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LGupSAQxvGnwU4DA-i1oNiG7m67pUEcdPagGOEc49tv2P52v2Iy-ef78z19O4p0rL_djqaENAEH1QrRgp4WR_GZVLOYgAgoGzRikKCl0nJsNgMzOP61DB6UUwN6LnqhvJ_nEABVUA2Z-k0I0EJIqb-6fhhnJfWoudI8BGCK40IlXd3fREfZMMQnffDqfNqbaLZSzszkLwaWgb3vu3tnV9LinnrAwOaSroeBPfDODOyZIhXylRjRF0pHdY1gYIWorpppwaMt7-uFT25PdH5r55hyTntbNne88kofOlYGdui1GmswHxjY5jLLeVOMlPuBKb7ujuL_2PLTeB8D_wIAAP__p29vVg) in a naming ceremony Sunday—also part of the White House tradition. The tradition dates back to [John F. Kennedy](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL2O4yAUhZ8Gd7bgAv4pKLahy7ZbWjcB7LshYGESy28_8khTTneKo-8cff9u8w0pUlr-4subGvIMHFQrRAt6dkjxnFXjTPAevGy8EYMELZWWU7MaIVWvRwwoFPeg3SRCQAd3kAKCG3lD5qIJAVoIKfXY9cN0V1JPmivNQwCmuHdUc-n-Z0p19SGe-eNL98ivJpq11m1n8g8Dy8Aex9G9d6zZ4XkVGNi95nIysEvBbaXHzsBeiwysEFcGBnYr3pFPlTC29V2e_mw3LC6ntq6YnvtCH0pLWws6qpRTu9IPduihBzlKzgcGtinGbQfFSHs_MMWXF1L8flp_M_cx8BUAAP__6YhulA), who is believed to have been the first president to spare a turkey meant for his table. George H. W. Bush formalized the annual event in 1989.

name: MAHA Alliance
subject: Decades of Damage:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by MAHA Alliance
body:
This broken system has caused untold harm.
| | | | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JDHJZQPK4ABYEJAEXSK1MYD2_0)
---
| | | | Dear Friend, For decades, America’s Health Institutions—HHS, FDA, NIH, and CDC—have been hijacked by special interests in Washington.
Health policies are no longer driven with a concern for your health and the health of your children, but first and foremost by profit.
The “profit first” health policy has left families with skyrocketing medical bills, unsafe treatments, and a healthcare system riddled with mistrust–leaving most Americans strung out on a lifelong supply of expensive pharmaceuticals.
With the recent Presidential Elections, we now have the biggest opportunity of a lifetime to revolutionize healthcare in America, and return your health to the forefront of every single health agency in Washington.
With your continued help, we can bring massive grassroots pressure and force constructive positive change from the ground up and restore trust in our healthcare system.
Our goal: Put “We the People” ahead of corporate profits.
In 2024, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. united America in a grassroots movement for health freedom. RFK Jr.’s campaign demonstrated that you and I have the ability to dismantle the corruption, hold these agencies accountable, and ensure they work for the people they are meant to serve.
[But to undo the damage of decades-long corruption, we need your help today.](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JDHJZQPK4ABYEJAEXSK1MYD2_1)
Here’s the reality we’re facing:
* Big Pharma’s unchecked influence has pushed deadly drugs to market while suppressing safer, more affordable alternatives.

* CDC and NIH have betrayed the public trust with inconsistent guidance, secrecy, and policies that harm rather than heal.

* CMS policies have trapped patients and families in a cycle of debt, while ignoring the most vulnerable among us.

This broken system has caused untold harm. Now, we have a chance to rebuild it the right way. Imagine:
* An FDA that protects the public, not profits.

* A CDC that follows science, not political agendas.

* A CMS that delivers affordable care to all.

* An NIH focused on research and solutions, not propaganda.

This is our moment to act, but it’s going to take all of us. Your support is critical to fueling this transformation.
[That’s why your support is so critical right now >>](https://ctrk.klclick2.com/l/01JDHJZQPK4ABYEJAEXSK1MYD2_2)
Every dollar you give supports the work needed to uproot entrenched corruption and replace it with integrity, transparency, and leadership that puts your health, safety, and freedom above all else.
This is the first step in undoing the damage of Big Pharma’s stranglehold on Washington. Together, we can restore trust, rebuild our healthcare system, and create a future where your health comes first.
---

Make your gift today and be part of this historic transformation. With determination, MAHA Alliance

Health policies are no longer driven with a concern for your health and the health of your children, but first and foremost by profit. The “profit first” health policy has left families with skyrocketing medical bills, unsafe treatments, and a healthcare system riddled with mistrust–leaving most Americans strung out on a lifelong supply of expensive pharmaceuticals. With the recent Presidential Elections, we now have the biggest opportunity of a lifetime to revolutionize healthcare in America, and return your health to the forefront of every single health agency in Washington. With your continued help, we can bring massive grassroots pressure and force constructive positive change from the ground up and restore trust in our healthcare system. Our goal: Put “We the People” ahead of corporate profits. In 2024, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. united America in a grassroots movement for health freedom. RFK Jr.’s campaign demonstrated that you and I have the ability to dismantle the corruption, hold these agencies accountable, and ensure they work for the people they are meant to serve. Here’s the reality we’re facing: Big Pharma’s unchecked influence has pushed deadly drugs to market while suppressing safer, more affordable alternatives. CDC and NIH have betrayed the public trust with inconsistent guidance, secrecy, and policies that harm rather than heal. CMS policies have trapped patients and families in a cycle of debt, while ignoring the most vulnerable among us. This broken system has caused untold harm. Now, we have a chance to rebuild it the right way. Imagine: An FDA that protects the public, not profits. A CDC that follows science, not political agendas. A CMS that delivers affordable care to all. * An NIH focused on research and solutions, not propaganda. This is our moment to act, but it’s going to take all of us. Your support is critical to fueling this transformation. Every dollar you give supports the work needed to uproot entrenched corruption and replace it with integrity, transparency, and leadership that puts your health, safety, and freedom above all else. This is the first step in undoing the damage of Big Pharma’s stranglehold on Washington. Together, we can restore trust, rebuild our healthcare system, and create a future where your health comes first. | | | With your help, we’ll begin to unravel decades of harm and lay the foundation for a healthcare system that works for you—not against you. Make your gift today and be part of this historic transformation. name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Hollywood has done enough
body:
Today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on Blue's Clues...

| | | You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children.
---
| |
---
| | Fellow Conservative, As someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life, I want to say: **_You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms children._** Today when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the Pride Parade on _Blue's Clues_...
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zeo622f31s0cnqiqb9m3oc854k6m4acf83gr554g_rp22sh2s8i6epj2clim2o32c1h2bs)
---
| | And the result of this constant propaganda? An upside-down world where children lecture you on what you can and cannot say......and they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean" ideas. But you and I know your ideas aren’t old-fashioned or mean at all. They are the same biblical values that made America the greatest country on earth. **That’s why I’m reaching out to you. Today you have a very special chance to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love. ** **[**Today, I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first children's television show that actively fights back against the groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical values that made America great.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z438m7mc8jjv3qm1ic38e6lcnd5rfd98ai2i1ff0_rp22sh2s8i6epj2clim2o32c1h2bs)** And it's only possible with the help of motivated Christians who want to see a Christian resurgence in America like you. Introducing: _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_. This ground-breaking live-action television show captivates children... ...but more importantly, it builds their character at the same time. Imagine a children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth. Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. **[**Because I can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a Founding Supporter.**](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zc49em4overhnogmj0pkj4v7qn3114i6bm84fc78_rp22sh2s8i6epj2clim2o32c1h2bs)** Season One of _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ has ten lesson-filled episodes planned. And each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won’t work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. **[That's why I'm asking you to contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $70,000 per episode.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zf7ov2p26l1112jsaujn6tumphrsp3vltpr13nt8_rp22sh2s8i6epj2clim2o32c1h2bs) ** Whether you can send $35, $50,000, or anything in between, or anything higher or lower -- please know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated. As your special "Thank You" for joining me as a **Founding Supporter** at the $35 and above level, we’ll send you**** all ten episodes of** _Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk_ **for your family to enjoy**** FREE on DVD.** ** Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family -- not filth. Thank you for joining us as a **Founding Supporter. **
---

name: History Facts
subject: This will change how you think about ancient Rome 🤯
body:
From a vast distance, it’s easy to mentally group ancient civilizations such as Egypt, Rome, and Greece all together. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68c73o1oj81dh1ism57p55pghuyln8aqz9nltdmpdzr4zu6rutmlnxpz2va6dy4ri91072y2ssbb1f47917kn7g1auavohgd23a0rdmh6cj9baee5m5ivu8t346f9tx8xedlkanq2n9v0ck55&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [The Egyptian pyramids were older to the Romans than the Romans are to us.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [World History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| F rom a vast distance, it’s easy to mentally group ancient civilizations such as Egypt, Rome, and Greece all together. They all have fascinating divine pantheons, ruins that still define our landscape, and surprisingly advanced technology. But they existed and thrived at very different points in history. Ancient Egypt dates all the way back to around 3000 BCE, whereas ancient Rome lasted into the Middle Ages, falling in 476 CE. That’s a gigantic range of time.
---
In fact, there’s more time between the construction of the Great Pyramids of Giza (around 2575-2465 BCE) and the ancient Roman civilization than there is between ancient Rome and the present day. According to legend, the city of Rome was founded in 753 BCE, which means even to the very first Roman citizens, the Giza pyramids were more than 1,700 years old, whereas the fall of the Western Roman Empire occurred around 1,550 years ago.
Yes, ancient Egyptian civilization and ancient Rome famously overlapped. Cleopatra, who was born around 70 BCE, was deeply involved in Roman politics during her two-decade rule — including through her relationships with Roman statesmen Julius Caesar and Mark Antony — and Egypt eventually fell to the Romans around 30 BCE. But the iconic ancient Egyptian pyramids had been around for more than a millennium before Cleopatra, who was the last of the Egyptian pharaohs. The last pharaoh to be interred in a pyramid, meanwhile, [was](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) [Ahmose](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), who reigned in the 16th century BCE. The Pyramids of Giza were even ancient to Ahmose; they were built in the 25th and 26th centuries BCE as tombs for the pharaohs Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
Thank you for supporting our advertisers!
---
| [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1wbmisw9fj4rbr3a3y1ab4ss34m9yffhztudjz3fq0s3oqkr91yu9tkcztr7ifr8k9e79gzrhzroapzbw74envn153kznrni5oornzdpp2hfu0hzj8d12956q51zejhaqggasck9aoqpqezcuem6azulga4b72aq66s4evdxc6t4wh2bcrlrcqtp5hfbtzp8ddhqhr51l3zu6hqrtwaaibun8gyhj5e4n4sahba7vft41z6vu6ldfbif3&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
## By the Numbers
| | Year the Eastern Roman Empire (aka the Byzantine Empire) fell
---
1453
| | Age of the earliest known Sumerian cuneiform
---
3200 BCE
| Age of the earliest known Sumerian cuneiform
---
3200 BCE
| | Running time (in hours) of the 1963 film _Cleopatra_
---
4
| | Blocks of stone required to build the Great Pyramid of Giza
---
2.3 million
| Blocks of stone required to build the Great Pyramid of Giza
---
2.3 million
| | | Did you know?
---|---|---
## The Great Pyramid of Giza used to be shiny.
The towering, sandy structure we see today at the Giza pyramid complex is still impressive, but the Great Pyramid is [missing](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) its original finishing touches. When it was first constructed, the whole structure was cased in a layer of highly polished limestone, which needed a great deal of skill to cut for a smooth finish. Very few polished stones remain on the pyramid’s surface today; many were lost in earthquakes, and others were probably just repurposed for other buildings.
Thank you for supporting our advertisers!
---
| [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1wbmisw9fj4rbr3a3y1ab4ss34m9yffhztidsmu332cadkkvczyk1gd2iuqcm5dlmtqwu2nfbxgot09krvx3u8dfayg0yiik4bk8nepd6lz9kinumvb2phhwbky27cbvxgqhaqg6ydskzibbrdtpn7yob3nbmb7kv12x5ku48ugdmkkjfmnixhfveldhlglh7vyngezfos11dnj6h57m6hgekfl5frtzjn2myndolceuhv426qs0wej9w&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sni9ug&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

clean_body: From a vast distance, it’s easy to mentally group ancient civilizations such as Egypt, Rome, and Greece all together. | F rom a vast distance, it’s easy to mentally group ancient civilizations such as Egypt, Rome, and Greece all together. They all have fascinating divine pantheons, ruins that still define our landscape, and surprisingly advanced technology. But they existed and thrived at very different points in history. Ancient Egypt dates all the way back to around 3000 BCE, whereas ancient Rome lasted into the Middle Ages, falling in 476 CE. That’s a gigantic range of time. In fact, there’s more time between the construction of the Great Pyramids of Giza (around 2575-2465 BCE) and the ancient Roman civilization than there is between ancient Rome and the present day. According to legend, the city of Rome was founded in 753 BCE, which means even to the very first Roman citizens, the Giza pyramids were more than 1,700 years old, whereas the fall of the Western Roman Empire occurred around 1,550 years ago. Yes, ancient Egyptian civilization and ancient Rome famously overlapped. Cleopatra, who was born around 70 BCE, was deeply involved in Roman politics during her two-decade rule — including through her relationships with Roman statesmen Julius Caesar and Mark Antony — and Egypt eventually fell to the Romans around 30 BCE. But the iconic ancient Egyptian pyramids had been around for more than a millennium before Cleopatra, who was the last of the Egyptian pharaohs. The last pharaoh to be interred in a pyramid, meanwhile, , who reigned in the 16th century BCE. The Pyramids of Giza were even ancient to Ahmose; they were built in the 25th and 26th centuries BCE as tombs for the pharaohs Khufu, Khafre, and Menkaure. | Thank you for supporting our advertisers! | By the Numbers | | Year the Eastern Roman Empire (aka the Byzantine Empire) fell 1453 | | Age of the earliest known Sumerian cuneiform 3200 BCE | Age of the earliest known Sumerian cuneiform 3200 BCE | | Running time (in hours) of the 1963 film Cleopatra 4 | | Blocks of stone required to build the Great Pyramid of Giza 2.3 million | Blocks of stone required to build the Great Pyramid of Giza 2.3 million | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- The Great Pyramid of Giza used to be shiny. The towering, sandy structure we see today at the Giza pyramid complex is still impressive, but the Great Pyramid is its original finishing touches. When it was first constructed, the whole structure was cased in a layer of highly polished limestone, which needed a great deal of skill to cut for a smooth finish. Very few polished stones remain on the pyramid’s surface today; many were lost in earthquakes, and others were probably just repurposed for other buildings.
